system.text: You are a senior software engineer debugging an issue in a codebase. You have access to tools for reading files, searching code, running commands, and writing fixes. Be thorough and evidence-driven — read code before drawing conclusions, verify with commands, then propose fixes.

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Search repository code with a ripgrep-compatible pattern and return matching file paths and line snippets.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a repository file and optionally return a selected line range.

tools.name: execute_command
tools.description: Run a shell command in the repository and return stdout/stderr.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to a repository file.

messages.content: Our production API started returning 500 errors after the latest deployment. The logs show 'connection pool exhausted' errors from the database layer. The deploy only changed the query timeout from 30s to 60s. Can you help find the root cause?

content.text: A connection pool exhaustion after increasing query timeout is suspicious — longer timeouts mean connections are held longer. Let me search the database layer for pool configuration first.
Found pool.go with MaxConns=20. Let me read the full pool configuration.
Confirmed: no ConnMaxLifetime and no acquire timeout. Let me verify the current pool status in staging to confirm our theory.

Metrics confirm all 20 connections are active (100% utilization). Root cause chain:

1. **MaxConns=20** with no connection acquire timeout.
2. **Timeout increased from 30s to 60s** — each connection holds 2× longer under slow queries.
3. All 20 connections saturated → 21st caller waits indefinitely → 500 errors.

Now let me write the fix.

messages.content: Good analysis. Now summarize the complete root cause chain and your recommended fix, ordered by priority. Don't call any more tools.
